Draft Day (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $28,842,237Details
International Box Office $1,005,243
Worldwide Box Office $29,847,480
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,628,237
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,562,619
Total $12,190,856
Further financial details...

Synopsis

On the day of the NFL Draft, general manager Sonny Weaver has the opportunity to save football in Cleveland when he trades for the number one pick. He must quickly decide what he’s willing to sacrifice in pursuit of perfection as the lines between his personal and professional life become blurred on a life-changing day for a few hundred young men with dreams of playing in the NFL.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$9,783,603 (33.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.95 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:96.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,781 opening theaters/2,781 max. theaters, 3.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $42,221,934

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 11th, 2014 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Singapore)
June 19th, 2014 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
June 27th, 2014 (Wide) (Vietnam)
July 3rd, 2014 (Wide) (Portugal)
July 10th, 2014 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
Video Release: September 2nd, 2014 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 on appeal for brief strong language and sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2305, 1/15/2015)
Running Time: 109 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Gratuitous Cameos, One Day, Split Screen
Plot point: Football, Underdog
Social setting: Corporate Life
Subgenre: Sports Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Summit Entertainment, OddLot Entertainment, Montecito Picture Company
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

On the day of the NFL Draft, general manager Sonny Weaver has the opportunity to save football in Cleveland when he trades for the number one pick. He must quickly decide what he’s willing to sacrifice in pursuit of perfection as the lines between his personal and professional life become blurred on a life-changing day for a few hundred young men with dreams of playing in the NFL.
